title: Amiloride: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking amiloride, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist. The following nonprescription products may interact with amiloride: potassium supplements or potassium-containing medication supplements; nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) such as ibuprofen (Advil, Motrin, others), and naproxen (Aleve, Naprosyn, others). Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking amiloride. Do not start any of these medications while taking amiloride without discussing with your healthcare provider. tell your doctor if you have kidney disease. Your doctor may tell you not to take amiloride. tell your doctor if you have or have ever had diabetes or liver disease. tell your doctor if you are pregnant, plan to become pregnant, or are breast-feeding. If you become pregnant while taking amiloride, call your doctor immediately. What special dietary instructions should I follow? 
Avoid potassium-containing salt substitutes while you are taking this medication. Talk with your doctor about the amount of potassium-rich foods (e.g., bananas, prunes, raisins, and orange juice) that you may have in your diet.
